tweets.text: "The allegation is that this teleprompter was placing bets on Kalshi. Think about it, a teleprompter gets the text from the president's speech writing or comms team before it's public" "So the allegation is that this teleprompter was placing bets on Kalshi" "Kalshi picked up the irregular activity, fulfilling its DCM self-policing obligation, and now this individual is on leave"

tweets.text: RT @ErikVoorhees: There is a misconception that to enjoy open-source you need to "run your own AI on your own hardware." Hobbyists enjoy such things, 99% of the world doesn't. And the best open source models are vastly larger than what most peoples' hardware can plausibly run. Solution is easy: access any model as a service from https://t.co/HGUdedRs2j (every open and closed model available) Private, unrestricted. Get an API key from Venice, give it to your agent, and you're gtg with no manual setup of anything.

tweets.text: 🚨NEW: @Sen_Alsobrooks tells me the White House’s idea to have the Department of Justice enforce the Clarity Act’s ethics provisions is an “unserious offer” and says she would not support the bill if that is the only enforcement option. Senator @berniemoreno, who has been working with @SenatorLummis to secure an ethics agreement with the White House, told reporters last night that the current proposal would put the DOJ, rather than state attorneys general, in charge of enforcement. “But we’ll keep working from that floor to reach an agreement that holds us all accountable,” Alsobrooks said. Alsobrooks is one of two Democratic senators who voted to advance the Clarity Act out of the Senate Banking Committee in May, and has said her continued support for the bill hinges on strong ethics provisions being included in the final text.
tweets.summary: 🚨NEW: @Sen_Alsobrooks tells me the White House’s idea to have the Department of Justice enforce the Clarity Act’s ethics provisions is an “unserious…

tweets.text: RT @abigbluebird: Duelling is the solution. Yeah sure, we could end with a similar outcome but odds are most times, there'll be a party that recognizes such petty disputes are not worth risking your life for and forfeits his honour/apologizes. A common nonsensical line that people spout about is that we as civilized society members can always talk things out. Show me a person who genuinely believes that and I'll show you someone who has never accomplished anything in life. It's just a total lack of basic understanding of human psychology. That only works if there's common ground/objectives. But most petty disputes are over 'feeling wronged' or perceived issues regarding 'justice'. It's just a gap of social etiquette in most cases that's not covered by the law. And barring cases of being oblivious or misunderstandings, as someone on the receiving end facing someone who's unwilling to 'yield', it's just either 'sucking it up', or doing a paggro war of intimidation which doesn't work on many elderly folks who've internalized that as long as they don't break the law, there's nothing you can do. The other way which the government has been very obviously dialling back on, is getting a lot more heavy on enforcement for social etiquette infractions. Before someone throws out the North Korea/China accusations, Germany and Switzerland can get pretty handed with noise complaints and dishing out fines. If the state wishes to hold a firm monopoly on the use of violence/force, it comes with a responsibility to 'right' social wrongs as much as possible. Worth noting that although it's almost never used, HDB has the power to seize back a unit as a last resort of a variety of owner infractions.
tweets.summary: RT @abigbluebird: Duelling is the solution. Yeah sure, we could end with a similar outcome but odds are most times, there'll be a party that…

tweets.text: For the past year, I have worked diligently to get the Clarity Act passed, fulfilling President Trump’s vision to make the U.S. the crypto capital of the world. Last week, it was reported that I was set to leave for mandatory training as part of my service in the Georgia Army National Guard, right before Clarity hits the Senate floor. While I remain committed to fulfilling my service obligation, I am grateful to report that my training has been deferred, and that I will be able to see this effort through to the end. Thanks to @POTUS and @DavidSacks for the opportunity to continue this important work and to everyone who reached out last week. Let’s finish the job. 🇺🇸
